title: Postmortem Politics
premise: You led a blameless postmortem for the biggest outage of the year, but now the VP pressures you to name a scapegoat before the board meeting. Your integrity, the team's morale, and your career hang in the balance.
setting: A tech company headquarters, meeting room. Present day. The outage caused $2M in lost revenue and public embarrassment. The VP and board want accountability.
goal: Navigate the political pressure to protect your team's blameless culture while satisfying the VP's demand. Success: present a constructive report with systemic improvements, no named scapegoat, and get buy-in from VP and key stakeholders. Failure: a scapegoat is fired, team morale collapses, or you are demoted.
gm_notes: The VP is a former engineer turned executive; he understands blameless culture but is terrified of the board. Introduce mild pressure first, then escalate. Possible complications: a rival manager leaks a false narrative, a junior engineer confesses out of guilt, the board chair demands a head. Pacing: 3-5 rounds of conversation / decision points.

roles.name: Emily Chen
roles.description: Lead SRE, female, 34, respected for technical skill and calm under pressure. You ran the postmortem.
roles.private_info: You discovered a config change by a junior engineer (Mark) triggered the outage, but the root cause was poor testing infrastructure. You want to protect Mark and fix the testing gap.

roles.name: David Park
roles.description: VP of Engineering, male, 50, ambitious, suits up for board meetings. Your direct boss.
roles.private_info: You are under immense pressure from the board chair to show decisive action. You privately believe in blameless culture but need a name to offer—maybe Mark, whom you consider replaceable.

roles.name: Sarah Lopez
roles.description: Director of QA, female, 45, pragmatic and team-oriented. She values process over people.
roles.private_info: You have been gunning for a promotion and see this outage as a chance to push for more QA resources. You suspect the testing infrastructure is underfunded, but you'll back the VP if it helps your cause.

npcs.name: Mark Thompson
npcs.description: Junior engineer, male, 26, anxious and guilt-ridden after his config change caused the outage.
npcs.hidden_agenda: He wants to confess and take responsibility to relieve his guilt, even if it means being fired.

npcs.name: Rita Sharma
npcs.description: Board chair, female, 62, retired CEO, known for being ruthless about accountability.
npcs.hidden_agenda: She wants a symbolic firing to satisfy shareholders and believes the VP is too soft. She is secretly grooming another exec to replace David.

npcs.name: James Okafor
npcs.description: Senior engineer, male, 38, mentor to Mark, respected by the team.
npcs.hidden_agenda: He knows the real root cause is an automated deployment tool that was never properly tested. He will leak this to the press if a scapegoat is fired.
